recognized_artifact: Scientific method / hypothesis-testing cycle (generic form, closely resembling abductive-hypothetico-deductive reasoning)

loops.exit_condition: Sufficient confidence reached in one explanation, or further investigation no longer justified

candidate_missing_primitives: Falsify (operation of actively attempting to disconfirm an explanation, distinct from general Evaluate)
ambiguities: The text does not specify whether 'one or more possible explanations' at the outset constitutes a single Generate event or an initial set later supplemented by additional Generate events during the loop — treated here as the same primitive recurring.

It is unclear whether 'examined to determine how well each explanation accounts for observations' is best represented as Interpret, Evaluate, or both in sequence; both were included to preserve uncertainty.

The termination condition ('sufficiently supported... or additional investigation no longer justified') could map to either Decide or a combination of Decide and an implicit Verify; no explicit verification-against-independent-criteria step is described, so Verify was not included.

No explicit Bound statement appears in the text beyond 'an unresolved situation or unanswered question is identified' — this was mapped to Bound, but the text does not describe scope-setting in detail.
notes: The blinded text describes a minimal, generic cycle: identify question -> generate candidate explanations -> gather evidence -> interpret/evaluate evidence against explanations -> loop (regenerate explanations or gather more evidence) until sufficient support or diminishing returns. No explicit Communicate, Allocate, Coordinate, Schedule, Sequence, or Synchronize primitives are present in the text; the process is single-actor and purely epistemic, with no resource-allocation or multi-agent coordination content described.
